title: alcea kurdica var. laxiflora
intro: Alcea kurdica var. laxiflora, also known as the loose-flowered hollyhock, is a plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae) that is native to Turkey and Iraq. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Alcea kurdica var. laxiflora include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
